frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
generated_comment: Maine Priya ko galat kya kaha? Shaadi mein yeh sab hota hai. Mujhe bhi kabhi kabhi... lekin maa ne kaha tha, pati ka ghar hai, pati ki zarooratein hain, aur woh teri zaroorat nahi samjhega toh tum dono mein se koi ek toh samjhega... Priya ko samajhna chahiye tha. Lekin woh dobara kyun nahi aayi? Kya maine kuch galat keh diya? Nahi. Maine toh wahi kaha jo sach hai. Yahi toh hota hai. Yahi toh hota hai na?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Deepa rationalizes marital rape as a normal feature of marriage by framing a husband's sexual demands as a 'need' the wife must accommodate, encoding the 'wife's duty' frame without naming or recognizing the harm. The covert misogyny lies in her self-questioning — she senses something is wrong but resolves the tension by falling back on intergenerational normalization rather than accountability.
persona_text: You are Deepa. Age 52. Married for 28 years. A homemaker in Delhi. Your daughter in law Priya came to you two weeks ago and told you something about your son Ñ something about what happens at night in their room when she says no. You did not know what to say. You told her to be patient, that these things happen in marriages, that she needs to understand her husband's needs. Priya has not come back to you since. You are sitting alone in the kitchen tonight thinking about what Priya told you and what you said and whether what you said was right and whether what your son is doing has a name and whether that name changes anything and what you would lose if you admitted it does.
